phrasalVerbs.definition: To become popular or fashionable; to understand something after initially not understanding it.
examples: The new trend caught on quickly among young people.
It took me a while, but I finally caught on to what she meant.
Streaming services caught on faster than anyone predicted.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To begin to realise or understand something, especially after being slow to do so.
examples: She eventually cottoned on to the fact that they were joking.
It took him ages to cotton on to the subtle hints.
The audience finally cottoned on to the comedian's dry humour.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To see, hear, or understand someone or something with difficulty; to suggest or imply something.
examples: I could barely make out what he was saying over the noise.
She made out that the project was her idea when it wasn't.
Can you make out the figures in this faded photograph?

phrasalVerbs.definition: To think carefully about something over a period of time.
examples: I need some time to mull over your proposal before deciding.
She spent the weekend mulling over her career options.
He mulled over the implications of the contract for several days.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To learn the truth about something by considering all the separate bits of information you have.
examples: Detectives pieced together the events leading up to the crime.
She pieced together what had happened from eyewitness accounts.
It took months to piece together the company's financial history.

fillInExercises.sentence: The new social media platform ___ very quickly among teenagers.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Catch on' means to become popular or fashionable.

fillInExercises.sentence: It took her a while to ___ on to what we were really discussing.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Cotton on' means to gradually understand something.

fillInExercises.sentence: I couldn't ___ what the sign said from this distance.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Make out' means to see or understand something with difficulty.

fillInExercises.sentence: Let me ___ over your suggestion and get back to you tomorrow.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Mull over' means to think carefully about something.

fillInExercises.sentence: The historian ___ the story from old letters and documents.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Piece together' means to understand something by combining separate information.

fillInExercises.sentence: Once the concept ___ on, everyone wanted to try it.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Catch on' can mean to become widely understood or popular.

fillInExercises.sentence: He ___ that he knew nothing about it, but I didn't believe him.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Make out' can mean to suggest or imply something.

fillInExercises.sentence: We managed to ___ together what happened from the CCTV footage.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Piece together' means to understand something by combining fragments of information.

lines.speaker: Alex
lines.text: Have you managed to [piece together|piece together] what happened at the event?

lines.speaker: Fatima
lines.text: Not entirely. I couldn't [make out|make out] most of the audio recording.

lines.speaker: Alex
lines.text: It took me a while to [cotton on|cotton on] to what the witnesses were saying.

lines.speaker: Fatima
lines.text: I've been [mulling over|mull over] the inconsistencies in their stories.

lines.speaker: Alex
lines.text: Once the pattern [catches on|catch on] in your mind, everything makes sense.

lines.speaker: Fatima
lines.text: Let me [mull over|mull over] this some more before we draw any conclusions.

lines.speaker: Chen
lines.text: This new app has really [caught on|catch on] with our target audience.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: I didn't understand it at first, but I eventually [cottoned on|cotton on].

lines.speaker: Chen
lines.text: Some users [made out|make out] that it was difficult to use initially.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: We [pieced together|piece together] user feedback to improve the interface.

lines.speaker: Chen
lines.text: Good. Let's [mull over|mull over] the next update before launching.
